Skip to contents
autotool 0.0.1
  • Get started
  • Reference

Build a tool-call handler from a function and pinned defaults

Source: R/make_handler.R
make_handler.Rd

Companion to generate_tool(). Returns a 1-argument closure that merges the supplied defaults under whatever argument list the LLM produced at tool-call time, then invokes fn. Drop the closure into your handler registry, call it with the model's parsed arguments.

Usage

make_handler(fn, defaults = list())

Arguments

fn

The function to invoke.

defaults

Named list of pinned arguments (typically the same list passed to generate_tool()).

Value

A function of a single argument (model_args) that returns whatever fn returns.

Details

The merge gives the model's arguments precedence: anything the model supplies for a key in defaults overrides the pinned value. That is usually correct — hidden arguments have defaults the model cannot see, so it has no way to supply them — but if you want strict pinning, validate before calling.

fn and defaults are forced at factory-call time, so reassigning the surrounding bindings later does not change the handler's behaviour.

See also

generate_tool().

Examples

add <- function(x, y, scale = 1) (x + y) * scale
handler <- make_handler(add, defaults = list(scale = 10))
handler(list(x = 2, y = 3))
#> [1] 50
# 50
